zotero.abstractNote: Learning accurate forward and inverse dynamics models is a fundamental challenge in robotics. While deep learning approaches have shown great success in learning complex dynamics, they often struggle with long-term stability and data efficiency. In this paper, we propose Action-Conditional Recurrent Kalman Networks (AC-RKNs), a novel architecture that combines the structural benefits of Kalman Filters with the representational power of deep neural networks. AC-RKNs are designed to learn forward and inverse dynamics models in a unified framework. By incorporating a latent state space that is updated via a Kalman filter-like mechanism, our model is able to capture long-term dependencies and maintain stability over long horizons. We demonstrate the effectiveness of our approach on a variety of robotic manipulation tasks, showing that AC-RKNs outperform state-of-the-art methods in terms of both prediction accuracy and data efficiency.

extra.Editors: Jens Kober and Fabio Ramos and Claire J. Tomlin

zotero.proceedingsTitle: Proceedings of the 4th Conference on Robot Learning
